response.justification: Sarah brings exceptional geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and Green Map projects, directly aligned with routing tile work. However, her last significant C++ work was in 2018 (6-7 years ago), and her recent career (2021-present) focuses on TypeScript/Node.js web development. While her track record demonstrates ability to master diverse languages, she'd require substantial ramp-up time to reach senior-level productivity in modern C++ systems programming—a risky gap for an immediate IC role, though her domain knowledge might justify consideration if the team can invest in technical mentoring.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise from founding and maintaining GISCollective platform, directly applicable to routing data pipelines
Proven ability to master diverse technical stacks and languages across web, embedded, and systems contexts over career
Real distributed systems exposure from Dunnhumby (micro-services handling millions of requests/sec) showing scalability thinking
concerns: C++ expertise is 6+ years stale (last work in 2018); recent career pivoted entirely to TypeScript/Node.js, creating significant technical debt in required primary language
No demonstrated experience with navigation routing algorithms, real-time traffic integration, or navigation guidance systems beyond general GIS work
Recent roles are high-level full-stack (TypeScript, React, AWS) rather than systems-level programming; unclear if can handle low-level performance optimization and data pipeline work
